items.title: Everything we like is a psyop
items.description: If culture is shaped by TikTok's algorithmic feed, then it's time for us to decide where we draw the line between necessary marketing and inauthentic growth hacking.

items.title: Google now lets you explore the web side-by-side with AI Mode
items.description: Now, when you're using AI Mode on Chrome desktop, clicking a link will open the webpage side-by-side with AI Mode.

items.title: Two Americans sentenced for helping North Korea steal $5 million in fake IT worker scheme
items.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced that two Americans were sentenced to years in prison for helping the North Korean government place fake IT workers in U.S. companies.

items.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
items.description: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong, it's diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is a part of it.

items.title: AI traffic to US retailers rose 393% in Q1, and it’s boosting their revenue too
items.description: Adobe says AI traffic to U.S. retail sites also jumped 269% in March, with visitors converting better and generating more revenue than non-AI shoppers.

items.title: Roblox’s AI assistant gets new agentic tools to plan, build, and test games
items.description: The new tools are designed to help creators throughout the entire development process.

items.title: Google adds Nano Banana-powered image generation to Gemini’s Personal Intelligence
items.description: That means its AI images can be created using Gemini's understanding of your likes and interests, without those having to be explicitly noted in the prompt.

items.title: Google is now targeting bad ads over bad actors
items.description: Google blocked 8.3 billion ads in 2025 but suspended fewer advertisers.

items.title: You’ve heard of hybrid cars. Now meet a hybrid cement plant.
items.description: Why choose fossil fuels or electricity when you can have both? NOC Energy has figured out how to hybridize heavy industry.

items.title: Runway CEO says AI could help Hollywood make 50 films instead of one $100M blockbuster
items.description: Runway’s CEO says AI could help studios make dozens of films for the cost of one, betting volume will boost hit-making odds.

items.title: Meta raises Quest 3 and Quest 3S prices due to RAM shortage
items.description: Starting April 19, the price of the Meta Quest 3S (128GB) and Meta Quest 3S (256GB) will go up by $50 to $349.99 and $449.99, respectively. The price of the Meta Quest 3 is going up by $100 to $599.99.

items.title: Canva’s AI assistant can now call various tools to make designs for you
items.description: The latest version of Canva's AI assistant lets users create editable designs with text prompts.

items.title: Fashion retailer Express left customers’ personal data and order details exposed to the internet
items.description: Retail giant Express was publicly spilling customer information to the open web. The bug is now fixed after TechCrunch alerted Express, but the company would not say if it plans to notify customers.

items.title: This simulation startup wants to be the Cursor for physical AI

items.description: Nuclear startup X-energy hit the road today to sell prospective investors on its initial public offering.

items.title: Ford EV and tech chief leaving automaker
items.description: Doug Field had stints at Apple and Tesla before joining Ford in 2021.

items.title: Wait, could they still actually break up Live Nation?
items.description: A federal jury found that Live Nation has acted illegally as a monopoly -- but the company just came to a tentative settlement with the DOJ last month.

items.title: Monarch Tractor’s collapse ends with an acquisition by Caterpillar
items.description: The startup had faced mounting pressure from dealers, farmers, and a co-founder who complained that Monarch's tech didn't work properly.
